contraventions.description: A licensee must not employ a person in a community care facility unless the licensee or, in the case of a person who is not the manager, the manager has first met with the person and obtained all of the following: a criminal record check for the person
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake March 30, 2026. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that criminal record checks had not been obtained for staff that were permitted to work with children.

contraventions.description: A licensee must ensure that the policies and procedures referred to in subsection (1) are implemented by employees.
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake March 30, 2026. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined staff members did not conduct a head count before leaving the outdoor area as outlined in their care and supervision policy, leaving two children without supervision.

contraventions.description: Without limiting subsection (1), a licensee must ensure that the ratio of employees to children attending a community care facility is no less than that permitted in Schedule E.
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake March 30, 2026. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that the Group Child Care (Under 36 Months) and Group Child Care (30 Months to School Age) programs did not meet the staff to child ratios permitted under Schedule E of the Child Care Licensing Regulation in the mornings. Multiple programs, including Dolphin, Sealion, Starfish, Jellyfish, Octopus, and Salmon were over ratio between March 2 and March 10, 2026.

contraventions.description: A licensee must ensure that children are supervised at all times by a person who is an educator, an assistant or a responsible adult.
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake March 30, 2026. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that on March 25, 2026, two children were left in the outdoor area without supervision.

contraventions.description: A licensee must do all of the following:(b) operate the community care facility in a manner that will promote the health, safety and dignity of persons in care
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake March 30, 2026. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that the terms and conditions of the licence were not followed, as the Group Child Care (Under 36 Months) programs frequently exceeded the licensed capacity during morning hours.

contraventions.description: A licensee must do all of the following:(b) operate the community care facility in a manner that will promote the health, safety and dignity of persons in care
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake October 7, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that staff did not demonstrate skills to promote health, safety and dignity of children in care when they did not respond promptly to children's severe illness symptoms, follow and implemented procedures and policies, notify parents and Licensing of reportable incidents.

contraventions.description: A licensee must ensure that the policies and procedures referred to in subsection (1) are implemented by employees.
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake October 7, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that policies and procedures in place were not implemented by staff.

contraventions.description: A licensee must immediately notify a parent or emergency contact if, while under the care or supervision of the licensee, the child becomes ill or is injured,
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake October 7, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that a child became ill while in care and parent was not notified immediately.

contraventions.description: A licensee must notify the medical health officer within 24 hours after (a) a child is involved in, or may have been involved in, a reportable incident described in Schedule H while under the care or supervision of the licensee,
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake October 7, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that a reportable incident was not notified to the Medical Health Officer within 24 hours.

contraventions.description: A licensee must not employ a person in a community care facility unless the licensee or, in the case of a person who is not the manager, the manager has first met with the person and obtained all of the following: a criminal record check for the person
contraventions.observations: Licensing audited 20 staff files and one file did not contain a criminal record check.

contraventions.observations: Regarding intake September 18, 2025. contraventions.observations: Regarding intake September 18, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that behavioural guidance practices were not age appropriate as staff were frequently physically moving children to guide behaviours.

contraventions.observations: Regarding intake September 18, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that a child was restrained by a staff member for behavioural guidance and it was not authorized by parents in care plan.

contraventions.observations: Regarding intake September 18, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that a staff restrained a child as emergency restraint due to challenging behaviours. The incident was not notified to the Medical Health Officer.

A licensee must ensure that all indoor and outdoor play materials and equipment accessible to children are safely constructed, free from hazards and in good repair.
contraventions.observations: Licensing observed five portable slides placed directly on concrete. The slides measured between 0.53 cm and 0.63 cm in height and were used by both the Group Child Care (Under 36 Months) and Group Child Care (30 Months to School Age) programs. The woodchips were unevenly distributed, and there was no depth to form a sufficient surface to reduce risk of injury from falls.

contraventions.description: A licensee, other than a licensee who provides a care program described as School Age Care on School Grounds, must ensure that any heated water accessible to children is heated to no more than 49° Celsius.
contraventions.observations: Licensing tested the bathroom sink where children access hot water and the temperature was 51.9 degrees Celsius. This represents an immediate risk to the health and safety of the children in care and must be corrected immediately. A corrective action plan was given by the manager at the time of inspection. The manager stated that the children will only use the cold water and the children would be supervised at all times and additionally notifying the Licensee. Full compliance will not be achieved until the plan has been implemented and completed.

contraventions.description: A licensee, other than a licensee who provides a care program described as School Age Care on School Grounds, must ensure that any heated water accessible to children is heated to no more than 49° Celsius.
contraventions.observations:
Licensing tested four washroom sinks and one classroom sink. Two of the sinks had water readings between 49 and 50.6 degrees Celsius. This represents an immediate risk to the health and safety of persons in care and must be corrected immediately. 

The plan for corrective action is to have staff monitoring children using the sinks in both the washroom and classroom. The Manager stated they would be requesting from the School Distract to have the water temperature lowered. Full compliance will not be achieved until the plan has been implemented and completed.

contraventions.observations: Licensing observed that the staff records upon request could not be retrieved within a reasonable time. The staff records (CRC's, resume's and references) were kept in a shared drive that Licensing did not have access to on site. The staff called the Licensee for the records and after time, the Licensee emailed the shared drive of records to Licensing. Licensing could not access internet and email during the inspection. The other half of the staff records (immunization status and certificates) are kept onsite. Licensing will come for further follow-up to correct areas of non-compliance of staff records.

contraventions.description: A licensee must ensure that, on the premises of a community care facility or in a vehicle used by employees to transport children, no one engages in restricted consumption while children are under the supervision of employees.
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake November 17, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that staff were vaping on the premises, including parking lot, bathroom and office.

contraventions.description: A licensee must do all of the following:(b) operate the community care facility in a manner that will promote the health, safety and dignity of persons in care
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake November 17, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that the Manager did not demonstrate the skills and abilities to carry out the duties as assigned.

contraventions.description: A licensee must do all of the following:(b) operate the community care facility in a manner that will promote the health, safety and dignity of persons in care
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake November 17, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that staff members did not demonstrate the skills and abilities to carry out the duties as assigned when they used a not an age appropriate behavioural guidance and did not implemented the policies.

contraventions.description: A licensee must ensure that the policies and procedures referred to in subsection (1) are implemented by employees.
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake November 17, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that policies were not implemented by staff members such as not smoking on the premises and behavioural guidance.

contraventions.description: Without limiting subsection (1), a licensee must ensure that the ratio of employees to children attending a community care facility is no less than that permitted in Schedule E.
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake November 17, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that the ratio of employees to children was less than permitted in Schedule E when Early Childhood Educator Assistants opened and closed the program longer than 30 minutes when an Early Childhood Educator was required in ratio.

contraventions.description: A licensee must ensure that children are supervised at all times by a person who is an educator, an assistant or a responsible adult.
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake November 17, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that children were not supervised at all times when staff members were sleeping in the nap room on multiple occasions.

contraventions.description: A licensee must ensure that behavioural guidance is appropriate to the age and development of the child who is receiving the guidance,
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake November 17, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that the behavioural guidance used by staff was not age appropriate to age and development when staff used loud and firm voices and physically guide children by holding their arms or wrists. Staff used harmful comments toward children as behavioural guidance.

contraventions.description:
A licensee must ensure that a child, while under the care or supervision of the licensee, is not subjected to any of the following: 
(c) harsh, belittling or degrading treatment by an employee or another child, whether verbal, emotional or physical, that could humiliate the child or undermine the child’s self respect
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake November 17, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that children were subjected to belittling, harsh, degrading treatment by staff members when they restrained a child and verbally expressed harmful comments at the child.

contraventions.description: A licensee must notify the medical health officer within 24 hours after (a) a child is involved in, or may have been involved in, a reportable incident described in Schedule H while under the care or supervision of the licensee,
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake November 17, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that multiple reportable incidents such as restraints and aggressive or unusual were not reported to the Medical Health Officer.

contraventions.description: A licensee must do all of the following:(b) operate the community care facility in a manner that will promote the health, safety and dignity of persons in care

contraventions.description: A licensee, other than a licensee who provides a care program described as School Age Care on School Grounds, must ensure that any heated water accessible to children is heated to no more than 49° Celsius.
contraventions.observations: Licensing measured two sinks in the girls washroom and two sinks in the boys washroom. The first sink measured at 46 degrees Celsius and the second sink measured at 51 degrees Celsius. The two sinks in the boys washroom measured at 50 degrees Celsius. The space is shared with a church and the church maintenance turned down the hot water, since then there has been many groups using the church space and it could have been potentially turned up. This represents an immediate risk to the health and safety of persons in care and must be corrected immediately. The staff stated the hot water will be turned off until the tap is repaired. Full compliance will not be achieved until the plan has been implemented and completed.

contraventions.description: A licensee must ensure that the policies and procedures referred to in subsection (1) are implemented by employees.
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake May 29, 2025. Additionally, staff were constantly physically moving children.

contraventions.description: Without limiting subsection (1) a licensee must ensure that children attending a community care facility are divided into groups, with each group having no more children than that permitted in Schedule E, (except FCC/IHMACC/REC; child qualified if in age range or within one month (except GCC School Age)).
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake May 29, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that children over the age of 36 months from the Group Child Care (30 Months to School Age) were moved to the Group Child Care (Under 36 Months), which did not comply with the ages permitted in Schedule E.

contraventions.description: A licensee must ensure that behavioural guidance is appropriate to the age and development of the child who is receiving the guidance,

contraventions.description: A licensee must ensure that a child, while under the care or supervision of the licensee, is not subjected to confinement or physical restraint by an employee, except as authorized in a child care plan if the plan includes instructions respecting behavioural guidance.
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake May 29, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that a child was restrained by a staff member without proper authorization in a care plan.

contraventions.description: If for any reason an employee described in Column 1 of the table in section 37 ceases employment, the licensee must ensure that within 30 days of the date the employee ceases employment, the licensee has sufficient staff to meet the requirements of section 34
contraventions.observations:
Licensing observed there were 19 children in care with three staff (ECE, ECEA, RA covering ECEA) in the Group Child Care (30 Months to School Age) program. Manager stated the ECEA ceased employment since January 2026 and they were covering the position with different substitutes. 
There were 10 children with three staff (ECE covering ITE, ECE, ECEA) in the Group Child Care (Under 36 Months) program. Manager stated the ITE staff ceased employment since November 2025 and they were covering the position with different substitutes. 
This did not meet the minimum requirements of Section 34 and Schedule E, and exceeds the 30 days since the employees ceased employment.

A staff at the Group Child Care (Under 30 Months) program observed the child was all alone in the hallway. Licensing Officer observed the child all along and told the staff in the Group Child Care (30 Months to School Age) program. The staff from the Group Child Care (30 Months to School Age) Program came out from the classroom and asked the child "Why are you here?". The staff stated she was handling the garbage bag and asked the child to line up and stay with her while they were forming a "special group" to take the garbage bag from the classroom to the bin in the hallway; however, the child ran away.

contraventions.description: A licensee must keep, for each child, a record showing the following information: (c) daily attendance record, indicating for each day whether the child is absent or, if the child is present, the time of arrival and departure
contraventions.observations: Licensing observed a record of daily attendance was not kept since October 8, 2025.

contraventions.description: A licensee must ensure that children do not have access to any object or substance that may be hazardous to the health or safety of a child
contraventions.observations: Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that the facility had mice problems and a mouse trap was placed in the nap room, there was mold in the facilities washroom were vitamins and a razor were accessible to children in care and the Licensee had a dog that urinated inside..

contraventions.description: A licensee, other than a licensee who provides a care program described as either Preschool (30 months to School Age) or Group Child Care (School Age), must have in a community care facility a sturdy surface for changing diapers, located outside the food preparation area, next to a covered container for soiled clothing
contraventions.observations: Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that there was no covered container in the washroom and soiled diapers were taken through the facility to the deck where the diaper pail was kept.

contraventions.description: A licensee must establish a program to instruct children in, and to practise the rules of, health and hygiene.
contraventions.observations: Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that the Licensee served food to a child without handwashing her hands or the child's hands and that the mattress cover used on a crib where children in care slept had food crumbs on it because a child had touched it with hands while eating.

contraventions.description: A licensee must immediately notify a parent or emergency contact if, while under the care or supervision of the licensee, the child is involved in, or may have been involved in, a reportable incident described in Schedule H.
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake February 2, 2026. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that [child A] was involved in a reportable incident as described in Schedule H on November 28, 2025. [Child A] was located in a washroom on their own. [Child A] was brought to a staff and returned to care. The Licensee did not immediately notify a parent or emergency contact of the incident where a child was missing or wandering.

contraventions.description: A licensee must notify the medical health officer within 24 hours after (a) a child is involved in, or may have been involved in, a reportable incident described in Schedule H while under the care or supervision of the licensee,
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake February 2, 2026. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that [child A] was missing or wandering, and it was not reported to the Medical Health Officer within 24 hours as required.

contraventions.description: A licensee must keep current records of each of the following: (f) a log of minor accidents, illnesses and unexpected events involving children, that did not require medical attention and were not reportable incidents described in Schedule H.
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake January 19, 2026. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that staff had observed [child A] push, kick, and hit other children. Staff verbally informed parents of the minor incidents [child A] was involved in. While the incidents did not meet the criteria for submitting a reportable incident, the Licensee was required to maintain records of all minor accidents, illnesses and unexpected events that involved [child A]. Licensing did not observe any associated documentation at the facility that [child A] had pushed, kicked, and hit [unexpected events].

contraventions.description: A licensee must do all of the following:(b) operate the community care facility in a manner that will promote the health, safety and dignity of persons in care
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake December 1, 2025, through the investigation process, Licensing determined that a staff member behaved disrespectfully toward coworkers on multiple occasions in the presence of children. Licensing confirmed two specific incidents that occurred. One was in the gym (exact date not recalled by staff), and another occurred on December 8, 2025, during which a staff member raised their voice and rolled their eyes at colleagues. The information gathered was consistent across sources and demonstrated conduct that was inappropriate and unprofessional in the child care setting.

contraventions.description: A licensee must ensure that behavioural guidance is appropriate to the age and development of the child who is receiving the guidance,
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake December 1, 2025, through the investigation process, Licensing determined that the facility had a policy requiring staff to communicate clearly and set limits with children in a positive manner. Licensing found that a staff member did not set limits in a positive way. The staff member self-reported that they used a 'bear hug' to move a child as a behavioural guidance technique. Using a 'bear hug' to move a child is not consistent with positive guidance or the facility's policy.

The area where the Licensee was sitting was located away from the kitchen where one child (age 3) remained indoors in the kitchen, seated in a highchair while eating. Licensing observed that from the Licensee's position outdoors, the child was not within line of sight and was not able to be heard due to the distance and noise of the other children playing. The Licensee stated the child was finishing their meal while the other children were playing outside.

contraventions.observations: Regarding intake August 12, 2025. contraventions.description: Without limiting subsection (1) a licensee must ensure that children attending a community care facility are divided into groups, with each group having no more children than that permitted in Schedule E, (except FCC/IHMACC/REC; child qualified if in age range or within one month (except GCC School Age)).
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake August 12. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined there one staff (ITE) with six children from 7:45 a.m. to 7:55 a.m. This is not meet the requirement of one infant and toddler educator and one other educator for 5-8 children for the Group Child Care (Under 36 Months) program.

contraventions.description: Without limiting subsection (1), a licensee must ensure that the ratio of employees to children attending a community care facility is no less than that permitted in Schedule E.
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake August 12, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined that the staff to child ratios were not maintained when there was one staff (ECE) with thirteen children in the Group Child Care (30 Months to School Age) program and Educator lunch breaks were being covered by a less qualified Educator.

contraventions.description: A licensee must ensure that behavioural guidance is appropriate to the age and development of the child who is receiving the guidance,
contraventions.observations: Regarding intake August 12, 2025. Through the investigation process, Licensing determined staff did not use appropriate guidance when staff moved children from under their arms and when children were being grabbed.
